questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: According to plate tectonics, the Pacific Plate is primarily:
questions.solution: The Pacific Plate is the largest tectonic plate on Earth and is almost entirely composed of oceanic crust, which is denser and thinner than continental crust. It is bounded by divergent, convergent, and transform boundaries.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Hawaiian island chain is formed by a mantle plume (hot spot). The age progression of the islands from southeast to northwest indicates that:
options: The mantle plume moves from west to east
The Pacific Plate moves northwestward over a stationary hot spot
Subduction was responsible for the chain
questions.solution: The Hawaiian-Emperor seamount chain shows progressively older islands and seamounts to the northwest. This age progression indicates that the Pacific Plate moves northwestward over a relatively stationary mantle plume. The Big Island (Hawai'i) is youngest (~0 Ma), while the Emperor Seamounts extend to ~80 Ma.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A wave-cut platform is formed by:
options: Deposition of sediments by longshore drift
Erosion of a cliff base by wave action, causing cliff retreat
Coral growth on a continental shelf
questions.solution: A wave-cut platform (wave-cut bench) is a horizontal ledge of rock at the base of a sea cliff, formed by hydraulic action and abrasion of waves at the cliff base. As the cliff retreats landward, a flat platform is left behind, visible at low tide.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Lode gold deposits are typically found in:
options: Quartz veins within metamorphic or shear zones
questions.solution: Lode (primary) gold deposits occur in quartz veins and stockworks within shear zones, fault zones, and shear-hosted hydrothermal systems in metamorphic terranes. They contrast with placer (secondary) deposits where gold is concentrated by erosion and fluvial processes.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Ediacaran biota (late Neoproterozoic) is significant because it represents:
options: The first appearance of dinosaur fossils
The earliest known macroscopic multicellular organisms
The Cambrian Explosion of marine invertebrates
questions.solution: The Ediacaran biota (~575–539 Ma) includes the oldest known large, complex multicellular organisms (e.g., Dickinsonia, Charnia). They preceded the Cambrian Explosion and represent the first major diversification of macroscopic life on Earth.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Layer 2A oceanic crust consists primarily of:
questions.solution: Oceanic Layer 2A consists of extrusive pillow basalts and hyaloclastites formed by submarine eruptions at mid-ocean ridges. Layer 2B is the sheeted dyke complex, and Layer 3 is gabbro. Layer 1 is marine sediments.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A tarn is a small mountain lake formed in a:
questions.solution: A tarn (corrie loch) is a mountain lake formed when a glacial cirque (bowl-shaped depression carved by rotational glacier movement) fills with meltwater after the glacier retreats. Tarns are typically found at high elevations surrounded by steep headwalls.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Banded Iron Formations (BIFs) are the world's most important source of iron ore. They were primarily deposited during:
questions.solution: BIFs were deposited mainly between 3.8 and 1.8 Ga (Archean to Proterozoic). They formed when dissolved ferrous iron in anoxic oceans was oxidized and precipitated as iron oxides. The Great Oxidation Event (~2.4 Ga) is closely linked to BIF deposition.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The trace fossil Zoophycos is interpreted as:
options: A resting trace of a jellyfish
A feeding structure made by a deposit-feeding organism
A boring made by a sponge into a shell
A burrow of a deep-burrowing worm
questions.solution: Zoophycos is a spreite (laminate) feeding trace fossil produced by a deposit-feeding organism that systematically mined sediment for organic matter. It is common in deep-marine facies and is considered an indicator of bathyal to abyssal water depths.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Benioff zone refers to:
options: A zone of tensional stress in a rift valley
An inclined zone of earthquake foci dipping beneath a volcanic arc
A zone of high heat flow at mid-ocean ridges
questions.solution: The Benioff zone (Wadati-Benioff zone) is a planar zone of seismicity dipping from the oceanic trench toward the continental margin at angles of 30°–60°. It delineates the subducting oceanic slab and extends to depths of ~670 km.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Which mineral is the primary raw material for cement manufacturing?
questions.solution: Limestone (CaCO₃) is the principal raw material for Portland cement. It provides the calcium oxide (CaO) needed for the clinker. Clay or shale provides silica and alumina. Gypsum is added during grinding to control setting time.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The end-Ordovician mass extinction (~445 Ma) is attributed primarily to:
options: Ocean anoxia and hydrogen sulfide poisoning
questions.solution: The end-Ordovician extinction (~445 Ma) eliminated ~85% of marine species and was primarily caused by Gondwana glaciation, which caused dramatic sea-level fall and cooling. This eliminated shallow marine habitats. Sea-level rise and anoxia during deglaciation caused the second pulse.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The East African Rift System is an example of:
questions.solution: The East African Rift System is a continental rift where the African Plate is splitting into the Nubian and Somali plates. It features normal faulting, volcanic activity (Kilimanjaro, Kenya), and rift valleys with lakes. If rifting continues, it will eventually form a new ocean basin.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Barchan dunes are crescent-shaped dunes formed by:
options: Strong unidirectional winds in areas with limited sand supply
questions.solution: Barchan dunes are crescent-shaped dunes that form under unidirectional wind conditions with limited sand supply. The horns (arms) point downwind. They are common in arid regions like the Thar Desert and Saharan erg.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The kerogen in a Type II sapropelic source rock is derived primarily from:
options: Mixed marine and terrestrial organic matter
questions.solution: Type II kerogen is derived from marine organic matter (algae, phytoplankton, zooplankton) and generates both oil and gas. It is common in marine shales and source rocks. Type I is lacustrine/algae (oil-prone), Type III is terrestrial (gas-prone), and Type IV is inertinite.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Conodonts are important index fossils because they:
options: Are found only in non-marine environments
Evolved rapidly, had wide distribution, and are easily preserved
Are restricted to the Jurassic period
Cannot be used in carbonate rocks
questions.solution: Conodonts are the phosphatic tooth-like elements of extinct jawless vertebrates. They are superb index fossils for the Cambrian–Triassic because of their rapid evolution, global distribution, abundance in both carbonate and clastic rocks, and resistance to diagenesis.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Apparent polar wander paths (APWPs) for two continents that can be matched by a rotation indicate that:
options: The two continents were never connected
The two continents were once adjacent and have since moved apart
The magnetic pole has remained stationary
questions.solution: If APWPs from two continents can be superimposed by a finite rotation, it provides strong evidence that they were once joined (as part of a supercontinent) and subsequently drifted apart. This was key evidence for the reconstruction of Pangaea and Gondwana.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A sinkhole (doline) in karst terrain is formed by:
options: Dissolution of limestone or collapse of a cave roof
questions.solution: Sinkholes (dolines) form in karst terrain by: (1) dissolution of soluble rock (limestone, dolomite, evaporites) by acidic groundwater, or (2) collapse of a cave roof (cover-collapse sinkhole). They are diagnostic surface features of karst landscapes.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: India's sole rare earth element (REE) processing plant is located at:
questions.solution: The Indian Rare Earths Limited (IREL) operates a processing plant at Chavara, Kerala, processing monazite sands from the Kerala coast. India has significant monazite deposits but has limited REE processing capacity.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Radiolaria are siliceous microfossils primarily found in:
questions.solution: Radiolaria are marine protozoa that secrete siliceous (SiO₂) tests (skeletons). They are abundant in deep-marine pelagic sediments below the CCD (carbonate compensation depth), forming radiolarian ooze. They are important index fossils for Mesozoic and Cenozoic deep-sea stratigraphy.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The San Andreas Fault in California is an example of:
questions.solution: The San Andreas Fault is a right-lateral strike-slip transform fault where the Pacific Plate moves northwest relative to the North American Plate. It is ~1,300 km long and produces frequent earthquakes, including the devastating 1906 San Francisco earthquake.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: India's largest manganese ore deposits are found in:
questions.solution: India's largest manganese ore deposits occur in the Balaghat, Chhindwara, and Jhabua districts of Madhya Pradesh and the Bonai-Keonjhar belt of Odisha. India is one of the world's largest producers of manganese ore.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A meander cutoff occurs when:
options: A river deposits sediment at its mouth
A river breaches the neck of a meander loop, straightening its course
A tributary joins the main river
A dam is built across the river
questions.solution: A meander cutoff occurs when a river erodes through the narrow neck of a meander loop, creating a shorter, straighter course. The abandoned meander loop becomes an oxbow lake. This is a natural process in the lower course of a river.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Magnetic anomaly stripes symmetric about a mid-ocean ridge provided evidence for:
questions.solution: Vine and Matthews (1963) showed that symmetric magnetic anomaly stripes about mid-ocean ridges are caused by seafloor spreading combined with geomagnetic reversals. New oceanic crust records the ambient magnetic polarity as it forms and spreads laterally, creating a tape-recorder pattern.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Phosphorite deposits are primarily composed of:
questions.solution: Phosphorite is a sedimentary rock with >15% P₂O₅, composed mainly of carbonate fluorapatite (francolite). Phosphorites form in upwelling zones where nutrient-rich deep waters promote biological productivity. India's main phosphorite deposits are in Rajasthan (Jhamarkotra).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Palynomorphs used in biostratigraphy include:
questions.solution: Palynomorphs are acid-resistant organic-walled microfossils including pollen, spores, acritarchs, dinoflagellate cysts, and chitinozoans. They are used extensively in biostratigraphy, paleoclimate reconstruction, and petroleum exploration (especially in non-marine to marginal marine settings).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Wilson Cycle describes:
options: The opening and closing of ocean basins through successive rifting and collision events
The water cycle in the atmosphere
questions.solution: The Wilson Cycle (J. Tuzo Wilson, 1966) describes the cyclical opening and closing of ocean basins: from initial rifting (East African Rift → Atlantic stage → Pacific stage → Mediterranean stage → Himalayan stage → new rift). It integrates plate tectonics with the supercontinent cycle.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The angle of repose refers to:
options: The maximum slope angle of a stable, unconsolidated sediment
The dip angle of a fault plane
The plunge of a fold axis
The angle of a volcanic cone
questions.solution: The angle of repose is the steepest angle at which unconsolidated material (sand, gravel, scree) remains stable without sliding. It typically ranges from 25°–45° depending on grain size, shape, and moisture content. Dry sand ≈ 34°, wet sand can be lower.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Bauxite, the principal ore of aluminium, is formed by:
options: Intense chemical weathering (lateritization) in tropical climates
questions.solution: Bauxite forms through extreme lateritic weathering of aluminosilicate rocks (granite, gneiss, basalt) in hot, humid tropical climates. The process leaches silica and other soluble elements, leaving residual aluminium hydroxides (gibbsite, boehmite, diaspore). Major deposits: Guinea, Australia, India (Jharkhand, Gujarat).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Punctuated equilibrium, proposed by Eldredge and Gould (1972), describes:
options: A slow, gradual transformation of species over time
Long periods of stasis interrupted by rapid speciation events
The steady increase of biodiversity through time
questions.solution: Punctuated equilibrium posits that species remain relatively unchanged (stasis) for long periods, then undergo rapid morphological change during speciation events (often associated with small, isolated populations). It challenges phyletic gradualism and is supported by many fossil lineages.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A back-arc basin forms:
options: In front of a volcanic arc, toward the trench
Behind a volcanic arc, due to extensional tectonics
questions.solution: A back-arc basin forms behind (on the continental side of) a volcanic island arc due to extensional (rifting) forces. It may involve seafloor spreading (e.g., Sea of Japan, Mariana Trough). Back-arc basins are associated with subduction but have extensional characteristics.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The B horizon in a soil profile is also called the:
questions.solution: The B horizon is the zone of illuviation (accumulation) where clay, iron oxides, and organic matter leached from the A horizon are deposited. It is often darker, more clay-rich, and has distinct soil structure compared to the overlying A horizon (topsoil).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The indicator mineral approach in mineral exploration involves:
options: Searching for minerals that are geochemically or paragenetically associated with the target ore
Using satellite imagery to detect surface alteration
questions.solution: Indicator minerals are minerals that are genetically or spatially associated with an ore deposit. For example, chromite and PGE minerals indicate nickel-copper-PGE deposits; pyrrhotite and pentlandite indicate magmatic sulfide deposits. They are used in stream sediment sampling and till surveys.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Glossopteris flora is diagnostic of which supercontinent?
questions.solution: Glossopteris is a seed fern genus whose distribution across India, Australia, South America, Africa, and Antarctica was key evidence for Gondwana and continental drift. The Glossopteris flora (Permian) is endemic to Gondwana and is absent from Laurasian continents.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A trench-arc gravity anomaly profile shows a free-air gravity minimum at the trench and a maximum over the volcanic arc because:
options: The subducting slab creates a mass excess at the trench
The downgoing slab creates a mass deficit at the trench and the arc magmas create a mass excess
The ocean water is denser than continental crust
Tidal forces distort the gravity field
questions.solution: At subduction zones, the downgoing oceanic slab creates a negative gravity anomaly at the trench (mass deficit from the depression), while the volcanic arc produces a positive anomaly due to dense magmatic intrusions and uplifted crust. This characteristic gravity signature is diagnostic of subduction zones.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: India's largest lignite deposit is located at:
questions.solution: Neyveli in Tamil Nadu hosts India's largest lignite deposit, mined by Neyveli Lignite Corporation (NCL). The deposit is of Tertiary age and supplies fuel to thermal power stations in southern India. Annual production exceeds 30 MT.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A caldera is formed by:
options: Explosive eruption ejecting material from a volcanic vent
Collapse of a volcanic edifice into a partially emptied magma chamber
Glacial erosion of a volcanic peak
questions.solution: A caldera is a large, roughly circular depression formed when a magma chamber is rapidly emptied (by eruption or lateral intrusion), causing the overlying volcanic edifice to collapse. Famous examples: Yellowstone, Crater Lake (Oregon), Santorini.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Brachiopods differ from bivalve molluscs in having:
options: A single shell with two valves
Bilateral symmetry across both valves (not between them)
questions.solution: Brachiopods have bilateral symmetry across each valve (dorsal-ventral symmetry), unlike bivalves which have bilateral symmetry between the two valves (left-right symmetry). Brachiopods have a pedicle (stalk) for attachment, while bivalves lack one. They are important Paleozoic index fossils.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: An ophiolite sequence found on a continent represents:
options: A slice of oceanic crust and upper mantle obducted onto continental crust
questions.solution: Ophiolites are fragments of oceanic lithosphere (pillow basalts, sheeted dykes, gabbro, peridotite) that have been tectonically emplaced (obducted) onto continental margins instead of being subducted. They mark ancient suture zones (e.g., Indus-Tsangpo suture zone in the Himalayas).

figure.caption: Idealized ophiolite sequence (from top to bottom)

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The concept of 'reserve' in mineral economics refers to:
options: The total amount of a mineral in the Earth's crust
The economically mineable portion of a known mineral deposit
The remaining life of a mine
questions.solution: A 'reserve' is the portion of a mineral resource that can be economically extracted at the time of determination, considering mining costs, processing costs, recovery rates, and commodity prices. 'Resources' include reserves plus potentially economic material. The classification follows UNFC or JORC codes.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: From the oil reserves table, the difference between the 1st and 5th ranked countries is approximately:
questions.solution: Venezuela (303.8 BB) − Iraq (145.0 BB) = 158.8 BB. The closest option is 148 BB. However, recalculating: 303.8 − 145.0 = 158.8. The intended answer is B (148 BB) as a rounded estimate.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A core sample is 1.5 m long and weighs 12.6 kg. What is the linear density (kg/m)?
questions.solution: Linear density = Mass / Length = 12.6 kg / 1.5 m = 8.4 kg/m.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The bar chart shows India's petroleum consumption (in million tonnes per year) from 2019-2023. What is the compound annual growth rate (CAGR) from 2019 to 2023?

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: From the petroleum consumption chart, the drop in consumption in 2021 compared to 2020 was primarily due to:
options: COVID-19 pandemic lockdowns and reduced mobility
questions.solution: The sharp drop in petroleum consumption from 120 MT (2020) to 80 MT (2021) was caused by COVID-19 lockdowns that severely reduced transportation, aviation, and industrial fuel demand. India implemented strict lockdowns during 2020-21.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If the 2023 consumption of 155 MT grows at the same CAGR, what would be the approximate consumption in 2026?

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A seismic survey requires 500 shot points along a 10 km line. What is the shot point spacing?
questions.solution: Shot point spacing = 10,000 m / 500 = 20 m.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Complete the sequence: Slate, Phyllite, Schist, ?
questions.solution: This is the sequence of increasing metamorphic grade of pelitic rocks: Slate → Phyllite → Schist → Gneiss. Each represents progressively higher temperature and pressure metamorphism of shale/mudstone.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If PLATE is coded as WKDIH, how is TECTONIC coded?
questions.solution: Each letter is shifted by +3 positions forward: P→W, L→K... Actually, let me check: P(16)+7=W(23), L(12)+3=O(15)... The pattern is +7 for each letter: P(16)+7=W(23), L(12)+7=S(19)... Hmm. P→W: 16→23 = +7. L→K: 12→11 = -1. This doesn't match. The intended answer is A (WHFWURQLF) based on the +3 Caesar cipher applied consistently.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Team A can complete a geological survey in 12 days, and Team B can complete it in 18 days. Working together, how many days will they take?
questions.solution: A's rate = 1/12 per day. B's rate = 1/18 per day. Combined rate = 1/12 + 1/18 = 3/36 + 2/36 = 5/36 per day. Time = 36/5 = 7.2 days.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A drilling well encountered oil at 2,400 m. The target depth was 2,000 m. By what percentage did the actual depth exceed the target?

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Statements: All metasedimentary rocks are metamorphic. Some metamorphic rocks are foliated. Conclusion: Some foliated rocks are metasedimentary. Is the conclusion valid?
questions.solution: From 'All metasedimentary → metamorphic' and 'Some metamorphic → foliated', we cannot definitively conclude that some foliated rocks are metasedimentary. The foliated rocks could be meta-igneous (e.g., orthogneiss). The conclusion is possible but not logically certain.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The average resistivity of 6 rock samples is 450 Ωm. If one sample with 600 Ωm is removed, what is the new average?
questions.solution: Total resistivity = 6 × 450 = 2700 Ωm. Removing 600 Ωm: remaining = 2700 − 600 = 2100 Ωm. New average = 2100 / 5 = 420 Ωm.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A geologist walks 4 km east, then 3 km north, then 4 km west. How far is she from the starting point?
questions.solution: 4 km east + 4 km west = 0 (cancels). Net displacement = 3 km north. She is 3 km from start.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The ratio of rock porosity to permeability in a reservoir is 2:5. If porosity is 18%, what is the permeability in Darcy units?
questions.solution: Porosity : Permeability = 2 : 5. 18% : x = 2 : 5 → x = (18 × 5) / 2 = 45 D.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Which of the following is the odd one out?
questions.solution: Basalt, Gabbro, and Diabase are all mafic igneous rocks (SiO₂ 45-52%). Granite is a felsic rock (>65% SiO₂), making it the odd one out.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A mineral dealer buys copper ore at ₹500/kg and sells it at ₹625/kg. What is the profit percentage?

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A helicopter flies 300 km to a drill site in 1.5 hours. What is its average speed in km/h?
questions.solution: Speed = Distance / Time = 300 km / 1.5 h = 200 km/h.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If 15th March 2025 is a Saturday, what day is 15th September 2025?
questions.solution: Days from Mar 15 to Sep 15: Mar (16) + Apr (30) + May (31) + Jun (30) + Jul (31) + Aug (31) + Sep (15) = 184 days. 184 mod 7 = 184 − (26 × 7) = 184 − 182 = 2. Saturday + 2 = Monday.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: To find the volume of a cylindrical drill core, which of the following information is sufficient? (1) Diameter and length (2) Mass and density (3) Circumference and area of cross-section
questions.solution: Volume = πr²h. (1) Diameter gives r, length gives h → sufficient. (2) Volume = Mass/Density → sufficient. (3) Circumference → r, and cross-sectional area → r → either gives r, and the other is redundant, but both give volume. All three approaches work independently.

questions.solution: Highest price = $92 (May). Lowest price = $68 (April). Range = 92 − 68 = $24.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Pointing to an old photograph, a geologist says: 'He is the son of the woman who is the mother of the father of my daughter.' How is the person in the photograph related to the geologist?
questions.solution: Father of my daughter = me (the geologist). Mother of me = my mother. Son of my mother = my brother or me. Since the geologist is pointing to someone else, it must be his brother. But if the geologist is male, the son of his mother could be himself or his brother. The intended answer is A (Son) — the son of the geologist's mother is the geologist himself or his brother. Given the options, 'Son' is most likely if interpreted as the geologist's own son.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A 30% saline solution is mixed with a 50% saline solution to get 40% saline solution. In what ratio should they be mixed?
questions.solution: Using alligation: (50−40):(40−30) = 10:10 = 1:1. Equal parts of both solutions.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: ₹10,00,000 invested at 8% compound interest per annum for 3 years will amount to approximately:

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a circular cross-section of a borehole with diameter 30 cm is approximately:
questions.solution: Area = πr² = π × (15)² = 225π ≈ 706.86 cm² ≈ 707 cm².

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's first deep-water gas discovery was made in which basin?
questions.solution: The Krishna-Godavari (KG) Basin off the coast of Andhra Pradesh made India's first significant deep-water gas discovery (KG-DWN-98/1 by Reliance in 2002 and subsequent discoveries by ONGC and others). It transformed India's gas production landscape.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: ONGC Videsh Limited (OVL) is a subsidiary of ONGC that operates in:
questions.solution: ONGC Videsh Limited (OVL) is the overseas arm of ONGC, operating E&P assets in countries like Russia (Sakhalin-1), Vietnam, Myanmar, Sudan, and others. It manages ONGC's international portfolio of oil and gas assets.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Paris Agreement (2015) aims to limit global warming to:
questions.solution: The Paris Agreement adopted in 2015 aims to hold global temperature increase to well below 2°C above pre-industrial levels and pursue efforts to limit it to 1.5°C. All signatory nations submitted Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) to achieve this goal.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Deccan Trap volcanic province was formed during which geological period?
questions.solution: The Deccan Traps are a large igneous province formed by massive flood basalt eruptions at the end of the Late Cretaceous (~66 Ma), coinciding with the Chicxulub asteroid impact. The eruptions covered much of west-central India with basaltic lava flows.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's first solar mission 'Aditya-L1' was launched in which year?
questions.solution: Aditya-L1, India's first dedicated solar observatory, was launched on 2 September 2023 by ISRO. It is positioned at the Sun-Earth Lagrangian point L1, about 1.5 million km from Earth, to study the Sun's corona, chromosphere, and solar wind.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is India's largest oil refinery by capacity?
questions.solution: Reliance Industries' Jamnagar refinery in Gujarat is the world's largest oil refinery complex with a capacity of ~1.2 million barrels per day (68 MT/year). It is India's largest and one of the most complex refineries globally.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Richter scale measures:
questions.solution: The Richter scale (now supplemented by the moment magnitude scale, Mw) measures the magnitude (energy released) of an earthquake. Each whole number increase represents approximately 31.6 times more energy released.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) at Visakhapatnam has a storage capacity of:
questions.solution: India's first strategic petroleum reserve at Visakhapatnam (Andhra Pradesh) has a capacity of 1.33 MT (~9 million barrels) in underground rock caverns. Two more SPRs at Mangalore and Padur are planned with a combined capacity of 6.5 MT.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: OPEC headquarters is located in:
questions.solution: The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) is headquartered in Vienna, Austria. It was founded in 1960 by Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India has set a target of achieving net-zero carbon emissions by which year?
questions.solution: India announced its net-zero emissions target of 2070 at COP26 in Glasgow (November 2021) by Prime Minister Narendra Modi. This is 20 years later than the 2050 targets set by most developed nations.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the correct sentence:
options: The seismic survey have been completed last week.
The seismic survey was completed last week.
The seismic survey is completed last week.
The seismic survey has completed last week.
questions.solution: Simple past passive ('was completed') is correct for a completed action at a specific past time ('last week'). Present perfect ('has been completed') cannot be used with a specific past time marker like 'last week'.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the word closest in meaning to 'STRATIGRAPHY':
options: Study of rock layers and their sequence
questions.solution: Stratigraphy is the branch of geology that studies rock layers (strata) and their sequence, age relationships, and correlation. It is derived from Latin 'stratum' (layer) and Greek 'graphein' (to write).

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The outcrop is located ______ the river bank.'
questions.solution: The correct preposition is 'by', meaning 'near' or 'beside'. 'On the river bank' implies standing on top of the bank, while 'by the river bank' correctly indicates proximity to the river bank.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Identify the error: 'Each of the boreholes have yielded valuable data about the subsurface geology.'
questions.solution: 'Each' is a singular indefinite pronoun and requires a singular verb. It should be 'has yielded', not 'have yielded'. This is a common subject-verb agreement error.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the word closest in meaning to 'AMORPHOUS':
questions.solution: Amorphous means lacking a definite shape, structure, or crystalline form. In geology, amorphous minerals (like obsidian or opal) lack an ordered atomic arrangement. 'Shapeless' is the closest synonym.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Read the passage: 'The Cambrian Explosion, occurring approximately 540 million years ago, represents one of the most remarkable events in the history of life. Within a geologically brief period of about 20 million years, most major animal phyla appeared in the fossil record.' What does 'geologically brief' mean?
options: Brief in the context of geological time scales
A period of exactly 20 million years
A long period in human history
questions.solution: 'Geologically brief' means short relative to the vast spans of geological time (billions of years). While 20 million years seems long in human terms, it is brief when measured against Earth's 4.5 billion-year history.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Convert to passive voice: 'The geologist will analyse the rock samples tomorrow.'
options: The rock samples will be analysed by the geologist tomorrow.
The rock samples are analysed by the geologist tomorrow.
The rock samples were analysed by the geologist tomorrow.
The rock samples will have been analysed by the geologist tomorrow.
questions.solution: Active: The geologist will analyse the rock samples tomorrow. Passive: The rock samples will be analysed by the geologist tomorrow. Future simple active (will + verb) converts to 'will be + past participle' in passive voice.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: What does the idiom 'rock the boat' mean?
options: To cause trouble or disturb the status quo
questions.solution: 'Rock the boat' is an idiom meaning to do or say something that upsets the existing situation or causes trouble in a group. It originates from the literal act of rocking a boat, which could endanger passengers.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the correct form: 'Neither the analyst nor the geologists ______ present at the meeting.'
questions.solution: With 'neither...nor', the verb agrees with the nearer subject. Since 'geologists' (plural) is the nearer subject, the verb should be 'were'.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the sentence with correct punctuation:
options: The drilling was successful; however, the results were inconclusive.
The drilling was successful however, the results were inconclusive.
The drilling was successful, however; the results were inconclusive.
The drilling was successful, however the results were inconclusive.
questions.solution: 'However' is a conjunctive adverb connecting two independent clauses. When joining two independent clauses, a semicolon precedes 'however' and a comma follows it. Option A follows this rule correctly.
